content:
1. This Act may be cited as the Panglima Gagah Berani
(Remembrance Allowance) Act 2001.
Interpretation

2.  In this Act, unless the context otherwise requires—
“child” includes a step-child and a child adopted by the recipient
under any written law relating to adoption, or under any custom or
usage;
“Decoration” means the Decoration of Panglima Gagah Berani
conferred under the Statutes of Panglima Gagah Berani (P.G.B.)
[G.N. 3151/1960];
“remembrance allowance” means the remembrance allowance
granted under subsection 3(1);
6                                        Laws of Malaysia                           ACT 616
“spouse” means the widow or widower of a deceased recipient
whose marriage is recognized in Malaysia as a valid marriage under
any written law, religion, custom or usage;
“recipient” means a person who has been conferred the Decoration.
Grant of remembrance allowance

3.  (1)  There may be granted to a recipient of a Decoration a
remembrance allowance of one thousand and five hundred ringgit a
month, and where the Decoration is conferred on such recipient more
than once, there may be granted to him a separate remembrance
allowance of one thousand and five hundred ringgit a month in
respect of each Decoration conferred on him.
(2)  The remembrance allowance shall be payable in arrear no
earlier than the end of each month.
(3)  Where
the
Decoration
was
conferred
before
the
commencement of this Act and the recipient is living upon such
commencement, the remembrance allowance shall be paid
retrospectively to the recipient, commencing with the month in which
the Decoration was conferred on him, but not earlier, in any case,
than the month of June 1983.
(4)  Where the Decoration is conferred on or after the
commencement of this Act, the remembrance allowance shall be paid
to the recipient commencing with the month in which the Decoration
is conferred on him.
Cessation of grant of remembrance allowance

4.  (1)  The grant of a remembrance allowance shall cease—
(a) upon the death of the recipient;
(b) upon the recipient being convicted for an offence by a
court of law in the Federation and sentenced to death, or
Panglima Gagah Berani (Remembrance Allowance)                 7
to
imprisonment
for
any
term
otherwise
than
imprisonment in lieu of a fine; or
(c) upon the cancellation or annulment of the conferment of
the Decoration in accordance with section VIII of the
Statutes of Panglima Gagah Berani.
(2)  No remembrance allowance shall be paid commencing with
the month in which the death of the recipient takes place, or the
sentence is imposed on the recipient, or the conferment of the
Decoration is cancelled or annulled, as the case may be.
(3)  For the purposes of subsection (2), the month in which the
sentence is imposed means the month in which the sentence is
pronounced by the trial court or if there is an appeal against such
sentence the month in which such sentence is confirmed on such
appeal.
(4)  If the recipient receives a free pardon in respect of such
conviction, or if the cancelled or annulled Decoration is restored to
him, the remembrance allowance shall be restored to him
commencing from the month in which the free pardon is received or
the Decoration is restored.
Grant to next-of-kin of recipient where Decoration is conferred
posthumously

5.  (1)  Where the Decoration has been conferred posthumously
before the commencement of this Act, there may be made a grant of a
total sum of fifteen thousand ringgit once only—
(a) to the spouse or spouses and children of the deceased
recipient who are living on the date of the commencement
of this Act;
(b) if on the date of the commencement of this Act no spouse
or child of the deceased recipient is living, to the parents
or parent of the deceased recipient who are or is living on
such date; or
8                                        Laws of Malaysia                           ACT 616
(c) if on the date of the commencement of this Act no spouse,
child or parent of the deceased recipient is living, to the
grandparents or grandparent of the deceased recipient who
are or is living on such date.
(2) Where the Decoration is conferred posthumously after the
commencement of this Act, there may be made a grant of a total sum
of fifteen thousand ringgit once only—
(a) to the spouse or spouses and children of the deceased
recipient who are living on the date of the conferment of
the Decoration;
(b) if on the date of the conferment of the Decoration no
spouse or child of the deceased recipient is living, to the
parents or parent of the deceased recipient who are or is
living on such date; or
(c) if on the date of the conferment of the Decoration no
spouse, child or parent of the deceased recipient is living,
to the grandparents or grandparent of the deceased
recipient who are or is living on such date.
Grant to next-of-kin of recipient who had died before
commencement of Act

6.  Where the Decoration was conferred before the commencement of
this Act and the recipient had died before the date of such
commencement, there may be made a grant of a total sum of fifteen
thousand ringgit once only—
(a) to the spouse or spouses and children of the deceased
recipient who are living on the date of the commencement
of this Act;
(b) if on the date of the commencement of this Act no spouse
or child of the deceased recipient is living, to the parents
Panglima Gagah Berani (Remembrance Allowance)                 9
or parent of the deceased recipient who are or is living on
such date; or
(c) if on the date of the commencement of this Act no spouse,
child or parent of the deceased recipient is living, to the
grandparents or grandparent of the deceased recipient who
are or is living on such date.
Prime Minister to apportion amount to be paid

7.  The amount to be paid to the spouse or spouses, children, parents
or grandparents, as the case may be, under section 5 or 6 shall be in
such proportions as may be determined by the Prime Minister.
Remembrance allowance and sum to be charged to the Federal
Consolidated Fund

8.  The remembrance allowance and sums granted under this Act
shall be charged to the Federal Consolidated Fund.
Remembrance allowance and sum not assignable, etc.

9.  Any remembrance allowance or sum granted under this Act shall
not be assignable, transferable, liable to be attached, sequestrated or
levied upon in respect of any claim.
10
